Aya Zaki is a Full Stack Java Developer with contact information including email (aya.zaki@email.com) and phone number (+20 101 234 5678), along with links to her GitHub (github.com/aya-zaki) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/aya-zaki) profiles, and personal website (ayazaki.com).

Aya Zaki received her M.C.A. from Pune University in January 2018, her B.C.A. from Amravati University in January 2015, her Higher Secondary Certificate from Amravati University in January 2012, and her Secondary School Certificate from Amravati University in January 2010.

From October 2018 to the present, Aya Zaki worked as a Java Developer at Salcluster Technologies LLP, contributing to web application development and utilizing technologies like Java Servlet, JSP, Ajax, HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and JQuery.

From May 2018 to September 2018, Aya Zaki was a Software Engineer at OmegaSoft Technologies Pvt. Ltd., developing project modules like Login, Registration, Role Assignment, and Payments using Java Spring Hibernate, AngularJs, Ajax, KendoUI, and Bootstrap, and applying MVC Architecture and Service-based Programming.

From January 2018 to April 2018, Aya Zaki interned as a Java Developer at Tech Solutions Inc. for the Employment Times project, developing the "Employment Times" website using Java, Spring, Hibernate, AngularJs, Ajax, and MySQL, and implementing modules like Admin Panel, E-paper Posting, Subscriptions, and a Payment module.
In 2017, Aya Zaki developed web applications for GST Billing, a news website (Dinman News Website), and an Agri Management Website, utilizing Java, JavaScript, Jquery, and MySQL.
Aya Zaki holds Java Developer Certificates from Unanth Technical Institute and SoloLearn, both obtained in 2018, and a Command Line Crash Course Certificate from Udemy, also in 2018.
The frameworks and libraries used include Ajax, AngularJS, and KendoUI, along with Spring and Hibernate.
The IDEs and tools employed are Eclipse, Spring Tool Suite, NetBeans, Sublime Text, and Atom.
The operating systems supported are Windows XP, 7, 8, and 10.
Aya Zaki's skills include programming languages like Java (Servlet, JSP, Spring Boot) and Core Java, and web technologies like HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and JQuery.
The architecture used includes MVC Architecture and Service-based Programming.

Aya Zaki is a dedicated and rapidly developing Full Stack Java Developer, readily accessible via email at aya.zaki@email.com, phone at +20 101 234 5678, and online through her GitHub (github.com/aya-zaki), LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/aya-zaki), and personal website (ayazaki.com). Her academic foundation is strong, built upon a Master of Computer Applications from Pune University (2018) and a Bachelor of Computer Applications from Amravati University (2015), preceded by her secondary and higher secondary education from the same institution.

Since October 2018, Aya has been a key contributor at Salcluster Technologies LLP, honing her skills in web application development with a versatile toolkit including Java Servlet, JSP, and modern front-end technologies like HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and JQuery. Prior to this, she gained valuable experience as a Software Engineer at OmegaSoft Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (May-September 2018), where she developed core modules – Login, Registration, Payments – utilizing Java Spring Hibernate and AngularJS. 

Aya’s proactive approach to learning is evident in her 2018 internships at Tech Solutions Inc., where she built the “Employment Times” website, and in her pursuit of certifications from Unanth Technical Institute, SoloLearn (Java), and Udemy (Command Line). Even before entering the professional sphere, in 2017, she independently developed web applications for GST Billing, a news website, and an Agri Management system, demonstrating a consistent drive to apply and expand her knowledge. She’s proficient in both Core Java and Spring Boot, and comfortable with database management using MySQL, consistently seeking opportunities to refine her skills and contribute to innovative projects.

Ajax appears as a consistently valuable tool in Aya Zaki’s toolkit as a developing software engineer. Not a person, but a technique – a quiet workhorse powering dynamic web experiences. From her internship at Tech Solutions Inc. building the “Employment Times” website, through her roles at OmegaSoft Technologies and currently at Salcluster Technologies, Ajax has been a constant companion. It’s the unseen element enabling responsive features like quick updates and seamless interactions within the applications she crafts. While often paired with frameworks like AngularJS and KendoUI, and alongside core technologies like Java, Spring, and Hibernate, Ajax isn’t merely *supported* by these; it *enables* them to function fluidly within the browser. It’s a foundational element, suggesting Aya Zaki understands the importance of building interactive and user-friendly web applications – a skill she’s honed and consistently applied across multiple projects and companies. It’s a core competency, a reliable method she returns to again and again, demonstrating its enduring relevance in her professional practice.
